figures.caption: Table 3: Performance Degradation (↓) of VLMs on VQA Task Under Multimodal Semantic Lighting Attacks Examples Generated from the COCO Dataset. We compare the correctness (%) evaluated by GPT-4 of previous methods with our proposed approach. The performance is considered better with lower values. Number in bold indicates the best performance.
Figure 4: Visualization of some digital MSLA samples causing LVLMs to give incorrect answers. In each LVLM response, we highlight the correct and error parts using different colors.

Table 2: Performance Degradation (↓) of VLMs on Image Captioning Under Multimodal Semantic Lighting Attacks Examples Generated from the COCO Dataset. We compare the consistency (%) evaluated by GPT-4 of previous methods with our proposed approach. Lower values indicate better attack performance. The best result is shown in bold.
Figure 6: Visualization of physical MSLA samples causing LVLMs to give incorrect answers. In each LVLM response, we highlight the correct and error parts using different colors.

Figure 1: Overall framework of MSLA. Given a clean image, we parameterize the triangular light by its relative position, radius, color, and three polar angles, and optimize these variables with a genetic algorithm. The optimized triangular light can be deployed using simple optical devices such as a flashlight, colored transparent sheets, and shape templates.
Figure 9: Attack performance under different transparency parameter 𝛼 with varying radius scaling factors 𝛾 on four CLIP variants.
